input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use the `IsTestProject` property, which `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` automatically sets to `true`. Place the MTP settings in a conditional `PropertyGroup` at the **bottom** of your `Directory.Build.props` (after the SDK has a chance to set the property):

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>

  <!-- shared properties for all projects -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- MTP settings – only for test projects -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>
```

**Why this works:** The `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package ships a `.props` file that sets `IsTestProject=true` early in evaluation. Any project that doesn't reference that package leaves the property empty, so the condition is false and your library projects stay as libraries.

**If `IsTestProject` evaluates too late** in your repo (rare), move the block to `Directory.Build.targets` instead — targets files are evaluated after all NuGet `.props`/`.targets` are imported, so the property is guaranteed to be set by then.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a knowledge question about MSBuild conditionals. The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks whether the project references `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` or has `IsTestProject` set, or to match on a naming convention.

The cleanest approach for MTP migration is to use the `IsTestProject` property, which is automatically set to `true` by the `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package. You can condition on that.


Now I'll provide a practical solution with the specific MSBuild syntax needed.
